title: Acidoton venezolanus
taxonomy: Acidoton venezolanus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Acidoton. Acidoton venezolanus is a small tree or shrub native to Venezuela. The species is part of the Euphorbiaceae family according to some classifications. However, there might be discrepancies due to variations in botanical classification.
uses: Acidoton venezolanus, also known as Pterocarpus officinarius, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine. Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
